text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听《Real Easy English》。

text: In this podcast, we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
zh: 在这个播客里，我们用简单的英语进行真实对话，帮助大家学习。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Beth. You can find a video version of this podcast
zh: 我是尼尔。我是贝丝。你可以在我们的网站上找到这个播客的视频版本，

text: and a free worksheet to help you learn on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 还有帮助你学习的免费练习题，网址是bbclearningenglish.com。

text: Hi Beth, how are you? I'm good, Neil, how are you? Very well, thank you.
zh: 嗨，贝丝，你好吗？我很好，尼尔，你呢？非常好，谢谢。

text: What are we talking about Today we are talking about housemates.
zh: 我们今天聊什么呢？今天我们聊的是室友。

text: Ah, housemates. The people that you share a house with who aren't your family.
zh: 啊，室友。就是和你同住一个房子、但不是你家人的人。

text: Maybe they're your friends or even strangers who just need somewhere to live.
zh: 可能是你的朋友，甚至是需要找地方住的陌生人。

text: Exactly. And it's quite common in the UK to live with housemates when you're younger.
zh: 没错。在英国，年轻时和室友一起住是很常见的。

text: Have you ever lived with housemates? Yes, of course.
zh: 你曾经和室友一起住过吗？当然有。

text: I had house or flatmates when I was a student
zh: 我上大学的时候有过室友或合租伙伴，

text: and then after I was a student in my first jobs and before I got married.
zh: 后来我毕业后开始工作、结婚之前也有过。

text: That's exactly the same as me. So when I went to university, I moved in with five people.
zh: 我也是一样。我上大学的时候，搬去和五个人一起住。

text: And we all had a separate room and a separate bathroom.
zh: 我们每个人都有自己的房间和独立的卫生间。

text: But yeah, they were my flatmates for my first year of university.
zh: 不过没错，那是我大一时候的合租室友。

text: But in my second year, then I moved into a house
zh: 但到了大二，我搬进了一栋房子，

text: and we all shared a bathroom and they were my housemates because we were in a house.
zh: 我们几个人共用一个卫生间，因为我们住在同一栋房子里，所以他们算是我的室友。

text: Did you enjoy having housemates?
zh: 你喜欢和室友一起住吗？

text: I think it really depends on the people that you live with.
zh: 我觉得这真的取决于和你住的人。

text: I have had some bad housemates. Bad because they
zh: 我遇到过一些不太好的室友。说他们不好是因为

text: weren't tidy, didn't clean, there were dishes in the kitchen that were dirty
zh: 他们不整洁，不打扫卫生，厨房里堆着脏盘子，

text: and not cleaned for a long time um but i've also had some really nice housemates as well
zh: 很长时间都不洗，不过我也遇到过一些非常好的室友。

text: do you enjoy living with housemates or did you enjoy living with housemates
zh: 那你喜欢和室友一起住吗，或者你曾经喜欢过吗？

text: i think as you said it really depends on the person
zh: 我觉得就像你说的，真的取决于人。

text: so you mentioned people being messy and that kind of thing
zh: 你刚才提到有人邋遢之类的，

text: also noisy easy people are difficult.
zh: 还有吵闹的人也很难相处。

text: But some of my best friends now have been my housemates in the past.
zh: 不过我现在最好的一些朋友，以前就是我的室友。

text: So I think you can really get to know people very well.
zh: 所以我觉得你真的可以借此非常了解一个人。

text: And you can also meet some people who you never want to see again in your life. That's true.
zh: 当然，你也可能遇到一些这辈子都不想再见的人。没错。

text: So, Neil, what do you think is the best kind of personality, the best kind of person to live with?
zh: 那尼尔，你觉得什么样的性格、什么样的人最适合一起住？

text: They have to be easygoing. What does that mean? Somebody who is easygoing
zh: 他们得随和。那是什么意思？随和的人

text: is relaxed and they don't get too upset or angry over little things.
zh: 就是比较放松，不会因为小事就生气或不高兴。

text: And they're quite easy to live with and there's the clue, easy going.
zh: 这样的人比较容易相处，关键就在于“随和”这个词。

text: I think something else important about living with
zh: 我觉得还有一件很重要的事，

text: other people or renting somewhere, so paying monthly, is having a good landlord.
zh: 不管是和别人同住，还是租房子，也就是每月付租金，那就是要有一个好房东。

text: Very important. So who is a landlord? What is a landlord?
zh: 非常重要。那房东是谁？房东是做什么的？

text: So the landlord is, or landlady, is the person that owns the house or flat
zh: 房东，或者说女房东，就是拥有你所住的房子或公寓的人，你要付他们租金。

text: that you're in and you pay them rent. So you pay them a monthly amount,
zh: 也就是说你每月付给他们一定的钱，

text: but you might live there for a year or two years or three years, but you don't own the place.
zh: 但你可能只住一年、两年或三年，房子并不属于你。

text: It's the landlord that owns the place. Yeah. And a good landlord can make a big difference.
zh: 房子是房东的。是的。一个好房东真的会带来很大不同。

text: Neil, are you easy to live with? Should we be flatmates?
zh: 尼尔，你适合一起住吗？我们要不要当合租室友？

text: I don't know. But I think
zh: 我不知道。不过我觉得

text: I have made some good friends when I've been a housemate.
zh: 我当室友的时候交到了一些不错的朋友。

text: So they think that I was a good housemate.
zh: 所以他们应该觉得我是个不错的室友。

text: Okay. How about you? Yeah, I think I'm easy to live with.
zh: 好吧。那你呢？嗯，我觉得我挺好相处的。

text: But I like to sing a lot. So maybe people would tell me to be quiet.
zh: 不过我很喜欢唱歌，所以别人可能会想让我安静点。

text: Although no one's ever told me to be quiet, but maybe they wanted me to, just didn't say.
zh: 虽然从来没有人真的叫我安静，但也许他们心里是这么想的，只是没说出来。

text: Let's recap the vocabulary we've heard in this podcast, starting with housemate.
zh: 让我们来回顾一下这期播客中学到的词汇，先从housemate（室友）开始。

text: A housemate is someone you live with who probably isn't part of your family.
zh: housemate指的是和你同住、但可能不是你家人的人。

text: We also hear flatmate. And we had easygoing, which means relaxed and not easily upset.
zh: 我们还听到了flatmate（合租室友）。还有easygoing，意思是随和、不容易生气。

text: Flatmates or housemates often rent a property
zh: 合租室友或室友通常是租房住，

text: and that means they don't own it, they pay money to the person who does own it.
zh: 这意味着房子不是他们的，他们要付钱给真正拥有房子的人。

text: And the person who owns the house or flat is the landlord.
zh: 而拥有这栋房子或公寓的人，就是landlord（房东）。

text: That's who you pay every month and they own the property. That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 你每个月付钱给他，房子归他所有。这就是本期《Real Easy English》的全部内容。

text: to test what you've learnt, there's a worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 想检验一下自己学到了什么，可以到我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上做练习题。

text: And we'll be back next week with another easy English conversation. Bye for now! Bye!
zh: 我们下周会带来另一期简单英语对话，再见啦！再见！
